title: canonical-castles record
description: One structure, resolved from its signals. The path data/sites/ is the frozen info-archetype record location (platform docs/archetype-contracts.md) even though this project's subjects are structures, not sites. The distinguishing field is `definitions_met`: this dataset never asserts that something IS a castle, only which named definitions it satisfies, each with a basis and each traceable to sources.

id.description: <country>-<place>-<name> per the id rule in data/profiles/records.md; must equal the filename stem.

name.description: Common name in the structure's own language, in Latin script.

name_local.description: Name in the local script where that differs (日本語, etc.).

tradition.description: The building tradition. ORTHOGONAL to the definition bands, deliberately: a Japanese shiro is not a lesser castle, it is a fortified lordly residence in a different tradition, and it takes the fortified_residence verdict on the same functional criterion as a European one. This field exists so that non-European structures are located rather than othered.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2. Never GB for a Scottish site's nation — use country: GB, region: Scotland.

region.description: Nation, state, Land, province, or département.

definitions_met.description: A verdict per definition band. Keys MUST be ids present in data/definitions.yml — enforced by scripts/check_integrity.py. A band may be omitted only when no source speaks to it; omission means 'unassessed', never 'no'. That distinction is the difference between this dataset and the one it was built to answer.

from.description: Year construction began, negative for BCE. Null when only a period is known.

period.description: Free text where years are unknown or contested: 'late 12th century', 'Iron Age'.

condition.description: `vanished` structures stay in the dataset: a destroyed castle is a record, not an omission. `reconstructed` matters because several bands turn on original fabric.

register_entries.description: Where this structure appears in official registers. This is what converts an individual record into evidence about a count.

ref.description: The register's own identifier for this structure.

designation.description: The class the register assigns it (Scheduled Monument, Grade I listed, Monument historique, …).

disputed.description: True when sources disagree about a band verdict for this structure. Set by the records gnome when it resolves a conflict; the conflict itself stays visible in the signals and in dispute_note.

dispute_note.description: What the disagreement is and who is on each side. Required when disputed is true (enforced by check_integrity, not schema).

confidence.description: Aggregate corroboration for the record as a whole.

sources.description: At least one. A record cannot exist without a citation — the schema enforces the honesty rule rather than trusting it.

verdict.description: MUST BE QUOTED IN YAML. `verdict: yes` without quotes parses as the BOOLEAN true under YAML 1.1 and fails this enum — write `verdict: "yes"`. Same trap as the archetype's all-dates-double-quoted rule. `contested` is a first-class outcome, not a failure to decide: it means competent sources genuinely disagree under this band's criterion. Counts report contested separately from yes — never silently folded into either.

basis.description: Why, against THIS band's criterion, in one or two sentences. 'It's obviously a castle' is not a basis. The basis must reference the criterion's terms.
